# **docs/visual-references.md**

## **Purpose of This Document**

This document contains **visual inspiration references** used to guide the design and layout of the Okelco website.

These references help ensure the UI remains:

* clean  
* premium  
* structured  
* minimal  
* consistent across pages

The reference website used for layout inspiration is **Tesla.com**.

Important:

Tesla is used **only as a design inspiration**, not as a brand template.  
The final website must reflect **Okelco's identity as a global tyre supplier**, not a car manufacturer.

# **Why Tesla Is the Visual Reference**

Tesla’s website is used as a reference because it demonstrates:

• Excellent layout rhythm  
• Strong visual hierarchy  
• Premium spacing and typography  
• Minimal but powerful UI structure  
• Clean navigation design  
• Smooth motion and transitions  
• Large high-quality imagery usage

These characteristics align with the goal of creating a **modern premium B2B tyre supplier website**.

Tesla’s structure is particularly useful for:

* hero sections  
* product showcase sections  
* feature storytelling sections  
* minimal navigation  
* strong CTA placement

# **Important Design Rule**

We are **not cloning Tesla**.

We are borrowing:

* layout structure  
* spacing rhythm  
* component hierarchy  
* animation style

But we must change:

* brand colors  
* imagery  
* typography tone  
* product focus  
* messaging

Tesla \= **layout inspiration**

Okelco \= **brand identity**

# **Reference Categories**

The screenshots below represent specific UI patterns we want to replicate in our own design system.

Each screenshot represents a **design behavior**, not an exact visual copy.

# **1\. Navbar Structure**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/navbar.png`

What this reference shows:

* Minimal top navigation  
* Centered navigation links  
* Simple logo placement  
* Icon utilities on the right  
* Clean hover states  
* Fixed navigation bar

Why we reference this:

Tesla’s navbar is extremely clean and avoids visual clutter.  
This structure works well for Okelco because it allows users to focus on the hero and product sections.

How we adapt it for Okelco:

* Okelco logo instead of Tesla  
* white background navbar  
* black text/icons  
* Okelco orange used for hover states and active links  
* language switcher icon retained

# **2\. Hero Section Layout**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/hero.png`

What this reference shows:

* full-width hero image  
* large bold headline  
* short supporting text  
* two CTA buttons  
* minimal overlay  
* arrow navigation  
* pagination dots

Why we reference this:

Tesla’s hero system is very effective at communicating a product story with minimal text.

How we adapt it for Okelco:

Replace car imagery with:

* tyre tread imagery  
* stacked tyres  
* tyre warehouse scenes  
* truck tyres  
* logistics tyre handling

Hero messaging should communicate:

* tyre sourcing  
* global supply  
* catalogue access  
* wholesale distribution

# **3\. Horizontal Product Carousel**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/product-carousel.png`

What this reference shows:

* horizontally scrolling product cards  
* large image cards  
* minimal text overlay  
* snap scrolling behavior  
* arrow navigation

Why we reference this:

This pattern works perfectly for displaying Okelco’s tyre categories:

* PCR Tyres  
* TBR Tyres  
* Used Tyres

It allows users to explore categories quickly without leaving the homepage.

# **4\. Feature Split Sections**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/split-section.png`

What this reference shows:

* large image on one side  
* text content on the other side  
* strong headline  
* supporting description  
* CTA buttons

Why we reference this:

Tesla uses this structure to explain product features clearly.

For Okelco we use this layout for:

* Used Tyres  
* TBR Tyres  
* Global Logistics  
* Trusted Brand Partnerships

This pattern creates strong storytelling while keeping the layout clean.

# **5\. Content Panel Sections**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/panel-section.png`

What this reference shows:

* large rounded panels  
* subtle background contrast  
* strong spacing  
* readable content blocks

Why we reference this:

This style allows us to present business information in a structured way.

We use this pattern for:

* Why Choose Okelco  
* Logistics capabilities  
* Supply advantages  
* Service highlights

# **6\. Trust / Certification Section**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/certification.png`

What this reference shows:

* centered content  
* large whitespace  
* single logo emphasis  
* trust messaging

Why we reference this:

Tesla uses similar structures for credibility and trust signals.

For Okelco this pattern is used for:

REX Certified information

This reinforces the company's credibility in international trade.

# **7\. Floating Utility Bar**

Screenshot reference:  
`/docs/visual-references/floating-bar.png`

What this reference shows:

* fixed utility strip  
* simple interaction element  
* clear call-to-action

Why we reference this:

This is adapted into a **quote request utility bar** for Okelco.

Example usage:

Left side:  
"Ask about tyre supply"

Right side:  
"Request Quote"

This provides quick access to customer inquiries.

# **Visual Adaptation Rules**

Even though Tesla is the structural inspiration, the Okelco site must visually reflect the tyre industry.

Therefore:

Use imagery of:

* tyres  
* tyre stacks  
* tyre warehouses  
* truck tyres  
* logistics transportation

Avoid excessive use of:

* luxury car imagery  
* sports car photography  
* automotive lifestyle shots

The focus must always remain on **tyre supply and logistics**.

# **Final Design Goal**

The finished website should feel like:

"A premium global tyre sourcing company website with the clarity and polish of Tesla’s interface."

It should communicate:

* professionalism  
* reliability  
* international supply capability  
* premium service quality

without appearing like a car brand.
